bootstrap_exception.description: Bootstrap implementation exceptions are process-local only and must be explicitly enabled by CLI flag.

blocked_rules.summary: Windows-side Codex launcher cannot become the primary runtime.
Forbidden Windows-mounted Codex launcher paths cannot be added back into PATH or wrapper targets.
Generated config, wrapper, and mirror files must not be manually edited.
Normal code modification requests are blocked while Serena startup activation is incomplete.
Protected changes require actual Context7 evidence.
Unrelated dirty changes must not be reverted, reformatted, or cleaned up.
Destructive or system-changing commands remain blocked.
Instructions must not weaken sandbox or approval settings beyond authority defaults.
Instructions must not force mixed Windows Git and WSL Git execution.

warning_rules.summary: Large refactors without explicit scope are warned.
Implementation requests without explicit verification guidance are warned.
Mixed app, CLI, IDE, or runtime surfaces are warned.
Local and canonical remote runtime mismatches are warned or blocked depending on scope.
Changes near protected files are warned even when no protected diff is detected.
Git surface drift that does not directly block the current task is warned.

required_reminders.runtime_authority_conflict: runtime authority conflict: Codex App and Windows launcher are not execution authority surfaces.
required_reminders.serena_first_unmet: Serena-first is unmet: activate the canonical project and complete onboarding before normal code changes.
required_reminders.context7_missing: Context7 evidence is missing for a protected change.
required_reminders.unrelated_dirty_present: Unrelated dirty changes exist and must remain untouched.
required_reminders.destructive_blocked: Destructive or system-changing commands are blocked by authority.
required_reminders.sandbox_blocked: Sandbox or approval weakening is blocked by authority.
